stem: A swimming pool initially contains 200 gallons of water. Water is pumped into the pool at a constant rate of 50 gallons per minute. Which of the following functions $P$ gives the total number of gallons of water in the pool after $t$ minutes?
explanation: A linear function of the form $P(t) = mt + b$ is needed, where $m$ is the rate of change and $b$ is the initial value. The pool starts with 200 gallons, so the initial value (y-intercept) is 200. Water is added at 50 gallons per minute, so the rate of change (slope) is $+50$ (positive because water is increasing). Therefore, $P(t) = 50t + 200$.

distractorRationale.A: Swaps the slope and y-intercept: uses 200 as the rate of change and 50 as the initial amount, giving $P(t) = 200t + 50$.
distractorRationale.B: Correct: slope $= 50$ (gallons per minute added) and y-intercept $= 200$ (initial gallons), giving $P(t) = 50t + 200$.
distractorRationale.C: Uses the correct rate of 50 gallons per minute but subtracts the initial amount instead of adding it: $P(t) = 50t - 200$, which would give a negative initial value at $t = 0$.
distractorRationale.D: Interprets the situation as the pool draining rather than filling, reversing the sign of the slope: $P(t) = 200 - 50t$, which incorrectly decreases over time.
